lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.

Gregory of Nazianzus is transferred to the See of Constantinople. The Emperor Theodosius falling Sick at Thessalonica, after his Victory over the Barbarians, is there baptized by Ascholius the Bishop.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Gregory of Nazianzus is transferred to the See of Constantinople. The Emperor Theodosius falling Sick at Thessalonica, after his Victory over the Barbarians, is there baptized by Ascholius the Bishop.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: By the common suffrage of many bishops, Gregory was at this time translated from the see of Nazianzus to that of Constantinople, and this happened in the manner before described.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: About the same time the emperors Gratian and Theodosius each obtained a victory over the barbarians.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And Gratian immediately set out for Gaul, because the Alemanni were ravaging those provinces: but Theodosius, after erecting a trophy, hastened towards Constantinople, and arrived at Thessalonica.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: There he was taken dangerously ill, and expressed a desire to receive Christian baptism.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now he had been instructed in Christian principles by his ancestors, and professed the ‘homoousian’ faith.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Becoming increasingly anxious to be baptized therefore, as his malady grew worse, he sent for the bishop of Thessalonica, and first asked him what doctrinal views he held?

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The bishop having replied, ‘that the opinion of Arius had not yet invaded the provinces of Illyricum, nor had the novelty to which that heretic had given birth begun to prey upon the churches in those countries; but they continued to preserve unshaken that faith which from the beginning was delivered by the apostles, and had been confirmed in the Nicene Synod,’ the emperor was most gladly baptized by the bishop Ascholius; and having recovered from his disease not many days after, he came to Constantinople on the twenty-fourth of November, in the fifth consulate of Gratian, and the first of his own.
